candidates.stmt:
I have worked and lived in the Worthing Area for the last 25 years.  For the last 10 years I have given all my free time to serving the local community, including 8 years as a Worthing Borough Councillor. As a councillor I was honoured to serve as the Worthing Mayor for 2 years and helped guide the town through the difficult covid period.

I was also the Borough’s Armed Forces Champion for 3 years, supporting our local service personnel and Veterans, this is a role I am proud to have held as a veteran of the Royal Navy myself. I continue to support our Armed Forces Community today, holding several positions with local associations. This is a major reason for me wanting to stand as your Prospective Parliamentary Candidate for the Reform Party in the East Worthing and Shoreham constituency.

Only Reform UK has clear fully costed policies to protect and strengthen our Armed Forces and Veteran Community, protecting our borders by controlled immigration, and safeguarding our children by banning Transgender ideology being pushed in our schools.

The Conservatives are tired and have run out of ideas, the Labour Party simply has no idea, and, as we have seen in Worthing, would not stand up for ordinary people, or our traditions, instead opting to rewrite history to suit the fringe minority groups.

It is time for our Country to have a Fresh Start, and only the Reform UK Party can deliver that, so please lend me your vote to enable all of us to thrive.
Sam Lovely is a Disabled and chronically ill father who is passionate about improving access for all to our local communities and services.

LABOUR SUPPORTS INVESTMENT in:

• Better buses and public transport
• Local jobs
• Affordable housing

Labour will protect our countryside and stand up for the National Park
The Labour Government has already committed £20 million extra investment to Courtwick – controlled by the local community.

Your Labour candidate is local resident Michael Ward. For a better deal for both Arundel and Courtwick, please vote MICHAEL WARD, LABOUR.

Website: https://southdownslabour.org.uk/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/southdownslabour
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/asd_labour/
Contact details: southdownslabour@gmail.com

Published and promoted by William Ashton on behalf of the Arundel & South Downs Constituency Labour Party and the candidate all at Toro, Lake Lane, Barnham PO22 0JB

© 2026 copyright Arundel & South Downs Labour All rights reserved.

Imogen Lysandrou has been fighting for fairness since she was 15, when she wrote an open letter to Michael Gove demanding arts funding be protected in schools.

Diagnosed with ADHD in 2010, she knows firsthand how neurodiverse people are let down by education and employment systems that weren’t built for them and she’s determined to change that.

She runs a not-for-profit modelling and talent agency that donates 50% of its income to local, grassroots and community groups; whilst advocating for ethical pay, fair working conditions, and better protections for workers in an industry that has long ignored them without regulations and protections.

Bersted is where she went to school, where she grew up, where her community is. She’s standing here because she knows it and she’s ready to fight for it.

Holly Smith (Bewbush & North Broadfield)  has lived in Crawley all of her life. She currently works for the NHS. She believes that we need to put community and the environment first, along with more investment in infrastructure and social housing.  Holly wants to make sure we have someone on the council to speak up for the environmental and social policies that the Green Party stands for. She says we must invest in our children’s future through things like renewable energy schemes, affordable housing, strong infrastructure (e.g. public transport) and reducing our carbon footprint as much as we can.

Mark's professional life of service includes the Police, National Crime Agency and international development "I have seen Billingshurst change dramatically in my 29 years living in the village, not all of it welcome. We have let development get out of control with no District Plan in place for the past 5 years. I want to see a properly worked through sustainable approach to any new Billingshurst homes - with improved local services and infrastructure"

Stephen works as a Pharmacy Technician at various Doctors surgeries. Experienced with healthcare settings and navigating the healthcare setting. As an enthusiastic electric car owner, he wants to work to make access to EV chargers for people at home much easier and simpler.

An avid Computer gamer but also concerned with how everything now requires a subscription and personal information that can put some people with disabilities at a huge disadvantage.

A family man who chose to move to the area over 8 years ago and felt passionate about getting involved to make a difference for Bognor Regis. With a varied career in IT, photography and management he brings with him a wealth of experience, an eye for detail and a leaning towards facts and statistical analysis. John will work hard to represent his constituents and always speaks, and votes, with them and their welfare in mind.

John believes we need to change to a fairer proportional representation system and supports the Make Votes Matter campaign.
Lived in Southbourne for over 20 years. Worked at Southbourne Library. Children attended local state schools. Has a keen interest in education and improving mental health awareness.

Antoine was born in France but moved to the UK in 2013, he recently became a British citizen and now lives in Southbourne.

He is a cabinet maker (and amateur gardener) who cares about the environment and the impact of climate change on the local community.

One of his main concerns is the number of new houses built without adequate access to local infrastructure, forcing residents to drive everywhere to get to school, shops or GP surgeries and contributing to congestion on the road. He is also concerned about sewage discharge in our waterways, and the levels of water abstraction from local rivers such as the Ems leading to wildlife loss.

I'm Victoria Finnegan, your local Green candidate for West Sussex County Council in the Bramber Castle division area. It's been wonderful getting things done for our community over the past years and meeting so many of you as part of my work for the local area. Thank you for your continued support.
I wanted to write personally because you face an important choice this May. The truth is, the old parties you've perhaps always voted for can't win round here this time.

I’m your local District councillor and I have the skills to do the job:

I was elected as Steyning & Ashurst's local Horsham District councillor in 2023 with the highest vote share. I love this place and the people, but I get fed up when our whole area from Ashurst through Steyning, Bramber, Upper Beeding, Small Dole to Woodmancote is ignored and neglected.

I want to make a real difference to your lives. I'm hard working, organised and I know how to get things done. As your West Sussex County councillor, I'll get even more done for our area.

I am always free to speak up for local residents:

Unlike other parties, the Green Party doesn't tell councillors how to vote. I can always put you ahead of party politics and stand up for what's right for Steyning, Ashurst, Bramber, Upper Beeding, Small Dole and Woodmancote.
As your HDC councillor, I've worked hard for safer roads, represented your concerns to Horsham District Council, sorted out overgrown footpaths and challenged developers to get a better deal for residents and the local environment. I've reported potholes and got them fixed. I've helped support securing Bramber Brooks as a local nature reserve.

I can win here in Bramber Castle division:

At the last local elections, the Greens won first place across the Steyning and Bramber areas. The Conservatives, Lib Dems and Labour don’t have enough support to win here, so it’ll be a clear, two-horse race between the Greens and Reform this time.

Simon Reed is a candidate that lives in the area.

He stands for:
- Ensuring new developments actually makes sense
- Protecting our environment - the nature here is genuinely special
- Getting our roads brought back to a good standard

He would welcome your vote - many thanks

I am standing in this election for the first time because I am horrified at the damage done to my country by Andrew Griffith and the Conservatives over the last ten years. I believe LibDems are best placed to beat the Conservatives in Arundel and South Downs. Liberal Democrats came second in this constituency in 2019. Last year we took control of both Horsham and Chichester District Councils and Labour did not win a single Council seat. If elected, I will work tirelessly to restore our NHS, to protect our environment and to improve our rural transport links. LibDems for a fair deal for all.

I'm your Green Party candidate for Horsham District Council in the Itchingfield, Slinfold and Warnham ward by-election and in the Broadbridge WSCC election.
You face an important choice when you go to vote on 7th May. That choice is between the same old parties doing the same old things, or having a representative
on the Council who really represents you. I'm standing against cuts to school funding, for adequate provision of school places and for proper funding for our NHS and social care. I'm standing against building thousands of homes across precious countryside without any of the infrastructure for the people who live there. I'm standing for a reliable rail service that enables people in Horsham to live and work, rather than just work and commute.

I’m local, hard-working, and I have the skills to do the job. You may recognise my name. In the last three general elections, I've been your Green Party parliamentary candidate. I've lived in Horsham District since I was two years old. I run my business here, I'm raising my kids here, I use local services and green spaces just like you do. 
As someone who grew up in one of Horsham's villages and who now lives in another one, I get fed up when our villages are ignored and neglected, so I'm standing up to do something about it.

I am always free to speak up for local residents. Unlike other parties, the Green Party doesn't tell councillors how to vote. I can always put you ahead of party politics and stand up for what's right for Barns Green, Itchingfield, Strood Green, Slinfold, Kingsfold, Warnham and of course Broadbridge.

We all know our area is being overdeveloped. Expensive new houses are built on green fields instead of genuinely affordable houses that local people can rent or buy. Instead of creating barriers across our town and its villages, I want to create opportunities.
After Sumners Fields in Barns Green, more houses are planned on a beautiful village field opposite the Queens Head, while the derelict brown

“I’m incredibly proud to be selected as a Labour candidate in Denne, my local ward for the local elections in May. I have been a member of the Party for over 30 years and I am also a member of Unite. I am a retired Property Solicitor and have been an active member in the Crawley, Tunbridge Wells, Wealden branches as well as this branch since moving here in 2014. I have stood as a Labour candidate in Denne and Southwater wards previously and look forward to standing again in Denne in 2019 in order to put forward a progressive socialist message. I am also an active member of Amnesty International and consequently have a keen interest in human rights issues. In my leisure time I am a keen reader as well as enjoying sport, although now just as a spectator rather than as a participant!”

Matt has lived and worked in Crawley for twenty-five years and the first two of his three daughters were born in Broadfield. He is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.

Professionally, he works as an engineer with experience including design, sales and quality assurance.

As a lifelong cyclist he has ridden a bike competitively, for leisure and as day-to-day transport; and he has ridden with and raced for the Crawley Wheelers.

I am proud to be standing as the Green Party Candidate in Broadwater, because I am passionate about making our community and town and greener, safer and fairer place for everyone. As someone who has lived in Worthing my whole life, I care so much about protecting our community and I want to ensure that our local area will continue to thrive for future generations.

Protecting our local environment and green spaces is incredibly important to me. I want to make sure residents have access to clean, safe and welcoming places where they can spend time with friends and family. That means protecting our beautiful parks from litter and anti-social behaviour as well as looking after our wonderful beach and sea and standing up to those pumping sewage into our waters.

I’m also passionate about improving road safety, supporting more social and genuinely affordable housing and strengthening the community spirit and diversity in Worthing. I also want to see more young people in politics who can bring in newer, fresh ideas and provide real change and real hope in Worthing

Richard has lived in Burgess Hill with his family for over 40 years. An experienced local councillor, he has been County Councillor for Burgess Hill East for the past five years.

Richard is passionate about ensuring that young people get the best start in life. He has been a governor for both secondary and primary schools in our area, including as Chair of Governors at the Burgess Hill Academy. Richard is a trustee of the newly re-opened Park Centre, with its long tradition of providing leisure facilities for young people.

Finally, Richard knows that local residents’ number one concern is the condition of our roads. Together with his fellow Lib Dem County Councillors, Richard has continually fought for increased emphasis and spending on better road maintenance and repair.

Colin lives and works in the area as a vehicle wrapper. His customers are mainly tradespeople who also work in the area. Colin is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.

Professionally, he has been in the vehicle wrapping and signage industry for over thirty years.

“As a local tradesperson, I understand the need for genuine representation and how the cost of living affects people. I am standing to give real people a voice. When I’m not at work or walking my rescue French Bulldog, Biscuit, I enjoy the best of what Burgess Hill has to offer—from our local theatre to our beautiful walking trails. I want to ensure these local treasures are preserved for the next generation.”

Jane has lived in Burgess Hill for nearly 40 years, raising two children here.

She has represented Burgess Hill North on West Sussex County Council since last year.

As your councillor, she has worked hard to support Burgess Hill schools and supported additional funding for pothole repairs.

Jane is a chartered accountant and helps run the Burgess Hill Youth Council.

She previously spent 12 years as a school governor at both Primary and Secondary Schools, including as Chair of Governors at The Burgess Hill Academy.

Ross has lived in Burgess Hill his whole life. He is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.

Professionally, Ross helps people in the area to overcome back pain and live happier, healthier lives. His experience includes:

Running a Fitness Studio near Burgess Hill Train Station for over 8 years
Supporting local people to improve their health, mobility and confidence through long-term coaching
Volunteering as a coach for Hassocks Juniors Football Club

“I’ve lived in Burgess Hill all my life and now i’m raising my kids kids here, I care deeply about the future of our town. I’m standing because I want young families to be able to afford to live well here, to enjoy our green spaces, and to feel proud of a town centre that’s thriving and safe. I’ll listen, work hard, and focus on practical solutions that make a real difference.”

I’m Pam Haigh your Labour Candidate at the 2021 West Sussex County Council Elections.

I have been a member of the Labour Party for over 20 years and have lived in Burgess Hill with my partner since 2006, since leaving corporate life I have become more active in my local Labour Party.

A former chair of Mid Sussex Labour, I have been involved in election campaigns at all levels and have previously stood as Labour candidate in County, District and Town council elections.

My work background is Project Management, Service Delivery and Service Improvement. If elected I will use those skills to deliver and improve local government services for the people of West Sussex.

Hi,

I'm Nathan Griffiths your pirate party UK candidate.

If elected I would fight for change, pressing for pothole repairs, modernisation of council IT systems, youth activities, increased transparency and the revitalisation of our town centre.

As a long standing resident of Chichester, I believe I understand local issues and local frustrations.

I have worked in private business for many years and my family have been through the schools, healthcare and local government institutions.

I want to cut waste inherent in the system, boost job opportunities through private sector investment, limit illegal immigration into the city, efficiently use public money to benefit the hard working majority and create a city to be proud of.

In my job I am responsible for creating and servicing a £3.5 million budget and I would like to use these skills to be used for the people of Chichester.

Thank you
Damian Purcell

Billy comes from the Midlands and moved to Chichester in 2023. He has been working in the online space for many years and spends his free time going on walks and writing, as well as being active in Green focused and LGBTQ+ communities.

On 7th May residents across West Sussex will head to the polls to decide who runs services at the County Council. Our community has a clear choice between the Liberal Democrats, who are focused on strong local services and supporting residents, and Reform UK, who have caused chaos where they run councils and have no clear plans for our area. After years of being let down by the Conservatives, with declining services and failing finances, it’s time for a fresh start that puts our community first.

I am proud to call Chichester South my home and have been working to deliver for residents and businesses across our community as a City Councillor. I care deeply about our community and understanding the challenges that we face, as a former Mental Health Trust Governor, I will advocate for our communities and make sure things are done properly.

I am committed to delivering strong, reliable local services: fixing our roads, building a community-focused social care system, and delivering a solution to local sewage discharge and over-development.

This is about choosing someone  local, experienced, and ready to fight for our area every day. If you want someone who will stand up for you and our community at
County Hall - then vote Liberal Democrat on Thursday 7th May.

Benedict was born in Chichester and grew up in Chichester South. He attended local schools, and went on to achieve a first class degree from Southampton shortly followed by his dream job.

Wanting to help people and local communities, he became a Parish Councillor and has been supporting local campaigns and communities. He has been volunteering at a local social hub, reporting potholes and pathway issues and has already been attending Full council meetings.

Benedict doesn’t mind getting muddy, clearing ponds that both make up local flooding infrastructure and provide a home for nature. Vote for Benedict For more hard work in Chichester South!

Jez  Sugars has lived in the Chichester area for eighteen years. Seven of those years in Fishbourne I trained in Bournemouth as a photographer at the Art institute, I have been in the photographic industry my whole adult life. I’m a practising Buddhist and a plant based environmentally conscious lover of nature with a keen interest in politics and fine art.

I have lived in the Offington Ward for nearly 30 years during which time I’ve enjoyed being involved with the local community and serving the Findon Valley Residents Association.  At the heart of the Green Party is an acceptance of all people and a desire to give them opportunity to enjoy life while not disadvantaging others.  The Green Party’s aim to create a more sustainable and fairer country by challenging some long held, but not working, approaches is both exciting and long overdue.

Lauren has lived in Mid Sussex for most of her life and made Cuckfield her home for the last 21 years.

Lauren is passionate about making our community a better place to live. From 2020 to 2023 she was the CEO of a charity supporting volunteering and community development in Mid Sussex, working to support over 450 charities and volunteer groups in our local area.

During the Covid-19 pandemic, she worked with both councils and voluntary organisations to support residents in need.

Lauren has consistently opposed building Cuckstye and will be a strong voice on the County Council for protecting green spaces. She will fight for safer roads, including fixing potholes, and work to get children and families a fair deal on SEND education.

Bob, 52, has lived in Mill Rd, Burgess Hill, for over five years. He was a Financial Adviser and Portfolio Manager for 25 years and has an extensive knowledge of the economy and finances. He has expertise in the science and technology behind climate change and is adept at explaining these complex matters in simple language.
Bob is a keen cyclist: in the last 12 months he has cycled 5,000 miles and driven just 350, backing up his commitment to stop our damage to the environment. He’s great at repairing bikes.

I became a member of the Green Party because I strongly believe in equality, community and the environment.

I have lived in Worthing for 13 years, working locally in health and social care. I currently work in the NHS as an Occupational therapist.

I previously worked in the homeless sector and  have seen first hand the impact of the lack of housing for local people- families and people with disabilities living in temporary accommodation out of area away from their families and social networks. We need to do more for our people unfortunately experiencing unstable housing.

I am also passionate about building community spaces in Worthing. I was a volunteer director and fundraiser for the alternative arts venue and social enterprise Cellar Arts Club. I also coordinated and hosted music events One Love Worthing, celebrating diversity and community support groups.

Key Issues I would focus on as a councillor:

- Housing for all – holding developers to account
- Community to build unity and opportunity
- Community wealth development- sourcing investment locally to build our economy.

I am standing because I strongly believe that people should have the opportunity to vote for a candidate who is committed to reducing social injustice and enhancing the natural environment. Since moving to East Grinstead I have come to value the many and diverse opportunities the town and surrounding countryside offer and I hope to be able to contribute towards protecting and improving it.

COVID-19 update to all Local Poll and Postal Voters for the  6th May 2021 Elections.
Please remember, although we are seeing a steady decline in the number of new cases both nationally and across the county, new cases are still being reported and national lockdown restrictions still apply.
Everyone should continue to follow the sensible measures in place to help prevent the spread of Covid-19, such as handwashing, social distancing and wearing face coverings. It is important that we all keep doing our part to prevent the spread of this virus.
And as a part to this I as a Independent Candidate standing in the fourth coming elections have decided  that to keep you all as safe as can be I shall not be knocking on your doors to gain your vote I shall miss seeing you all, but I feel I should put you heath first. I hope you can understand my reasons.
Kind Regards
Norman Mockford

Vanessa has lived in Sussex for over 20 years, the last 14 in East Grinstead. Vanessa is the CEO of a lobby group representing universities across the UK including the University of Brighton. Vanessa was the first in her family to go to university and is a committed campaigner for the right of every young person to have access to high quality tertiary education.

Vanessa joined the civil service from university and worked for 23 years in a variety of key government and non-government departments including employment, education, environment and Olympic / Paralympic sport. Vanessa has a child at one of the local senior secondary schools.

Vanessa will be a vocal advocate on the County Council for the 3 Es: education, employment and the environment.

Liz Hammond has lived in East Preston for over forty years, her children grew up here, and now grandchildren. Liz has a studio in her garden as when she is not doing other things she is in there painting. Liz joined Friends of the Earth at the end of the 1970s, and still is a member. Liz joined the Greens as well about 10 years ago.

Sue has lived in Barnham her whole life and has spent years fighting for the people who live here.

As a Parish and District councillor, she’s taken on flooding from raw sewage, fly-tipping, illegal tree-felling, light and air pollution, road damage, parking, and litter, the real, everyday issues that affect people’s lives.

She’s a fierce advocate for residents and a devoted animal-lover, and it shows in everything she does.

Fontwell already knows and loves Sue, now she’s standing to do even more.

Experience that Counts. Results that Matter.

Like you, I’ve watched our rural villages come under fire from inappropriate overdevelopment. I’m not just a resident; as the former Chairman of Aldingbourne Parish Council, I know how the system works—and how to fix it when it doesn't.

I’m standing for West Sussex County Council because Fontwell needs a seat at the table where the real decisions are made. While others might offer protests from the sidelines, I offer a plan to direct the £842 million capital budget toward the priorities that affect your daily life:

- Real Infrastructure, Not Just Rooftops: I will fight to stop development that lacks the necessary GP capacity, school places, and sewers. We need the A29 realignment finished, not more "insane" planning approvals that ignore local reality.
- Fixing the Floods: I’ll force agencies and water companies to finally work together to fix our flooding hotspots for good.
- A United West Sussex: I am committed to keeping our historic county together against those who wish to carve it up into smaller, less efficient authorities that waste your taxes on officer salaries instead of services.

The Choice is Clear: In this election, the math is simple. Splitting the vote with Reform only lowers the bar for a Green Party candidate who is more interested in national ideology than fixing a pothole in Eastergate.

Don't trade proven competence for a protest vote. Let’s keep Fontwell moving forward with a strong, local Conservative voice who knows how to deliver.

On Thursday, May 7th, Vote Dr Mario Trabucco.

VOTE FOR DEBORAH SEE AS YOUR COUNTY COUNCILLOR FOR FONTWELL

Deborah has lived in Barnham for over twenty years. She cares cares deeply about tackling the everyday challenges people in Fontwell Division face .  Having worked in the voluntary sector and as a teacher, she knows first-hand the difference high quality local services make to people’s day to day lives and their futures.

As your county councillor for Fontwell she will pursue these matters:

- Providing an integrated bus and rail system rather than building more roads.
- Improving the condition of lanes and roads.
- Putting in place traffic calming measures in villages.
- Ensuring effective flood management planning.
- Protecting the environment and countryside– ending sewage spills in villages, streams and rivers and stopping littering and fly tipping.
- Building affordable housing in the right place at the right price.
- Providing local services needed by the  community.
- Making streets safer through initiatives such as Sussex Safe Spaces.
- Representing the views of local people at county, district and parish level.

Read more at:

Website: https://southdownslabour.org.uk/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/southdownslabour 
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/asd_labour/

Contact details: southdownslabour@gmail.com

Published and promoted by William Ashton on behalf of the Arundel & South Downs Constituency Labour Party and the candidate all at Toro, Lake Lane, Barnham PO22 0JB

©️ 2026 copyright Arundel & South Downs Labour All rights reserved.

Erika lives in Hassocks with her husband and two children. She was elected as the County Councillor for Hassocks & Burgess Hill South in June 2025.

Erika is passionate about making our community a better place to live. She is the event coordinator for Light Up Hassocks and a trustee of Hassocks Community Organisation.

On the County Council Erika has stood up for better social services, road repairs and education.

If re-elected to the County Council Erika will continue to be a strong champion for our local area and community.

Di has lived in Hassocks since 2009.  She is committed to strengthening the local community and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.

Professionally, she is a freelance business consultant following a 25-year career in Digital Product Development and business transformation. Her experience includes:

Senior leadership in a large organisation
Developing strategy and planning it’s delivery in a variety of local charities/ non profit organisations and small businesses
Trustee/ Committee member in a number of local charities and groups

“I’m standing because I support hope! I believe it is all of our responsibility to protect and restore the environment for future generations, and to focus on what unites rather than that which divides us.  Our community deserves a safe and fair present and future.”

Kirsten has worked in education for over 25 years, teaching students from Year 9 to Sixth Form, preparing them for GCSE and A-level exams.

She has taught at schools and colleges both here in Haywards Heath and across Sussex. Kirsten knows that the County Council needs to do better for pupils.

As a member of the planning committee on Haywards Heath town council, Kirsten has been strong voice for the scrutiny of developers.

On the County Council, Kirsten will fight for better education, pothole and road repairs, and road safety.

I am standing as the Green Party candidate for Haywards Heath East on West Sussex County Council because I want to represent our community with a focus on education, young people’s wellbeing and a greener future. As a teacher, I see every day how important good schools, strong youth services and proper mental health support are for children and families. I want to ensure that young people in Haywards Heath East have the opportunities and support they deserve. I am also committed to safer roads, better public transport and protecting local green spaces so our community remains a healthy and sustainable place to live.

Caroline has lived in Haywards Heath for 22 years, raising two children here. Since last year she has served on the Town Council, where she has supported residents in protecting local green spaces.

She is the Vice-Chair on the board of FareShare Sussex & Surrey, working to tackle food poverty in our community.

Caroline has spent 35 years working in the technology sector and has a record of supporting community groups as a volunteer including the Guides, Brownies, Air Cadets and Age UK. She has also served as a local school governor.

Caroline will be a strong voice on the County Council for improving our roads, schools, and local services.

I’m standing because Haywards Heath deserves better. Residents are facing rising bills, local shops are disappearing and our environment is under pressure. It doesn’t have to be this way.

As an experienced public service worker, a union representative and a long term local political voice, I know that now is the time for change. Let’s lower bills, let’s support independent businesses, let’s protect our green spaces, and use the pride we have in our town for good!

I have lived in Partridge Green for 25 years, I've grown up here in the middle of Henfield's electoral division and am proud to call it home. I have connections with  Henfield, Cowfold, Shermanbury and Dial Post. Having studied Social Policy at university, since concluding my studies I've been involved in a number of parish councillor initiatives and personal activism supporting residents in the villages surrounding me, I would like to apply my experience and knowledge further to the role of county councillor at a crucial time in local government. I have learnt quickly that applying yourself at a local level, and mobilising residents at the right times can initiate change and move things in the right direction. This is one of those times. Being a servant of your community is incredibly rewarding. Being rooted in Sussex I am seeking to usurp this division's history of being solely Conservative and offer my neighbours genuine hope, accountability, and a willingness to work hard to represent and speak up for all of them.

Looking forward to serving Henfield, Partridge Green, Cowfold, West Grinstead, Wineham, all all the areas in between. Having done 20 years of parish council and school governance, I know how to get things done. Recently retired, I now have time to really commit to this important role for the next four years.

I've recently realised that everything I love doing is ultimately about community building. Whether a volunteer gardening group, a community sewing project, environmental education with Sussex Green Living in schools and at festivals, my long involvement as course leader for the charity Action for Happiness or volunteer warden for the YHA- it's all about helping create well informed, strong, supportive, healthy communities.  In my role as Chair of Horsham Trafalgar Neighbourhood Council I love working cross-party and non-party to achieve a happy, resilient community in as many ways as is possible given the limits imposed by the current political structures.

The Lib Dems have held Horsham Hurst for years. And that means they’ve stopped listening. I want to change that. I was raised in Horsham, educated locally, and have a strong understanding of the needs of our community. I’ve supported children who needed extra help through work as a volunteer tutor. I’ve worked closely with young people in care. Those experiences have shaped my commitment to our community. That’s why I’m campaigning to make our community
an even better place to live and work. That includes campaigning for a 20mph
speed limit on busy roads such as Rushams Road, which are being used as rat runs.

candidates.p: The Peace Party - Non-violence, Justice, Environment

candidates.stmt:
Rodney has lived in Hurstpierpoint for nearly 60 years and contributed to village life as a teacher, councillor and scout leader.

Now retired, Rodney was a Chemistry Teacher for 24 years and worked for HM Inspector of Health and Safety (Chemicals Division) for 18 years.

A long-serving member of Hurstpierpoint & Sayers Common Parish Council, Rodney was elected to Mid Sussex District Council in 2019. Since then, he has been a strong voice on the District Planning Committee for scrutinising developments in our area.

Rodney spent 26 years as Scout Leader/Group Scout Leader of the 1st Hurstpierpoint Scout Group. He is now retired from the role, but is still an enthusiastic supporter of the Mid-Sussex Scouts.

Sue has lived in Hurstpierpoint for 35 years and for most of her working life she was an educator of secondary age students and she says she loved every minute of the time spent doing this. Sue believes that our young people deserve the chances in life that people of her generation have experienced. But she fears that some of these opportunities are being closed down by the scale of the environmental and social justice issues that we face. She hears her grandchildren and their friends discuss their worries about their future. That’s why she joined the Green Party some time ago as they are the only political party actually committed to engaging with the realities of the energy crisis, the cost of living crisis, the climate crisis, the environmental crisis and working with realistic, sustainable policies.

“I sincerely believe that having more Green Party councilors elected will have a significant effect in moving local issues forward. We seek a collaborative approach rather than being combative. Planning needs a rethink, water supply and sewage treatment needs a rethink. Housing developments need a rethink, especially on higher standards of energy efficiency. There is so much that currently is just not being done. We see that central Government shows little sign of changing this situation. Action at local government level is needed to address the challenges predicted for our very near future

Currently politics is in turmoil as neither the Labour nor the Conservative parties are offering the solutions needed. Politics is failing us and it’s time for change. I can be a part of that change: I would work hard to represent you in all aspects of governance.”

candidates.p: The Official Monster Raving Loony Party

candidates.stmt:
Statement for WSCC Elections 2026
This election is for a Councillor to serve for the last two years of the County Council before it is replaced by a Unitary Authority in 2028. There will be separate elections for that council next year. Ensuring that there is a smooth transition into the new council, with no interruption to our local services, will be challenging.  As your current County Councillor, I am asking for your vote on the 7th May so that I can complete this important work.

If re-elected, I will continue to work for:
•	Proper repairs to potholes and resurfacing as an alternative to patching.
•	Better enforcement to stop fly-tipping, anti-social parking and speeding on rural    rat runs.
•	Reduced congestion at the Felbridge junction and along the A22.
•	Investment in the town centre and more effective use of the financial contributions from developers to provide new community buildings and sports facilities.
•	The relocation of Imberhorne Middle School to Imberhorne Lane
•	Timely completion of Special Educational Needs assessments and delivery of the support recommended
•	A strategic approach to new housing that respects our Neighbourhood Plans, delivers the necessary new infrastructure and protects our countryside and heritage buildings.

As an Independent Councillor I don’t have to follow a party line, I am free to decide every issue on its merits and to work with other parties to implement the policies and services which are in your best interests.

I grew up in Scarborough and came South to read Physics at the University of Kent.  I studied molecular liquids for my PhD using the experimental reactors at Harwell and the Institute Laue Langevin in Grenoble.  After completing my thesis I joined the MOD working on the development of new land and air systems at Fort Halstead and Farnborough.  My work included the development of combat simulations to support procurement decisions.  I became a Fellow of the Institute of Physics in 1999 and transferred to the

Born in the UK to South American parents, Andre spent many years overseas, and when he came back, East Grinstead became his home. For almost five years, he has lived in Imberdown with his wife and kids, building strong, genuine connections with the people and places here. He has spent these years working in education, teaching young people across secondary schools, which has deepened his commitment to supporting the community.

Professionally, he works as a Humanities Teacher. His experience includes:

• Secondary school teaching
• Working with SEND students
• Community involvement with Blackwell Primary School (supporting the PTA), Participation in East Grinstead Community and Leisure Centre, Volunteering with the local Scout, Regular attendance at town events

“My priority is to support our future generations and their environment, giving them hope in an achievable and realistic way, and strengthening their connections with our local communities, which are our pillars, especially in these uncertain times. I am committed to supporting our local schools by offering the help they need and acting as one of the intermediaries between them, governors, Mid Sussex Council, and West Sussex Council.

I am also focused on protecting our green spaces and promoting more community action to keep them safe, including much-loved areas like Worth Way. Improving opportunities for young people’s wellbeing is equally important to me, and I aim to work with local partners to increase access to leisure, sport, and safe environments. I also want to encourage more support and discussion sessions within our community to help improve young people’s mental and physical health.”

Nigel Sweet is a retired emergency NHS ambulance clinician who was also a governor of the regional NHS ambulance Trust SECAmb, and a senior trade union official for UNISON. Nigel was last on Adur Dictrict Council as Deputy Leader and Housing Committee Chair in 2000 when Labour held control for a short time. In a diverse working life, Nigel also worked for some years in the House of Commons as a political researcher for a Sussex Labour MP. He has been a wholefoods retailer and a design engineer in the microprocessor industry. In the Co-operative Movement, Nigel has held elected member roles regionally and nationally within The Co-op Group – formally the CWS.

I grew up in Adur and want to see decisions made here by normal people like myself, and make positive change in my local community. I have a particular interest in Transport, Equality and Culture. If elected I promise to be hard-working, easily accessible and be a voice for regular people who work and live in Adur.

Tahira is passionate about helping people and doing what she can to help make a difference to the town, having previously served Crawley as a councillor and Deputy Mayor.

She has lived in Crawley for almost her entire life, working in the town and raising her family here.

Tahira understands the problems facing many families in Langley Green and Tushmore right now due to COVID, having previously lost a job due to cuts at the airport. Since then she has played a role helping to end the pandemic, working for the NHS on tracking and tracing.

If elected, Karen will focus on ensuring equal opportunities for our neighbourhood’s children. Everyone knows that resources are scarce, but it seems that those with the greatest need are losing out. This needs putting right.

When times are tough, the seeds of division are often sown and take root more easily. We must not let our neighbourhoods become divided and local representatives, including councillors, must work with local groups to promote the understanding that underpins cohesion.

“Politicians have become complacent and our neighbourhoods have been neglected. Hard times should never be an excuse for doing nothing. I live in the area and everyday I see for myself big things and little things that could be done to make things better for ordinary people, if only the will was there. I have the will and I have the experience to know how to do it.”

Hello, I’m Kemuel Thompson, and I’m proud to be standing as your Liberal Democrat candidate for Langley Green & Tushmore (and Ifield East) in the upcoming election.

I’m standing because I believe our community deserves a strong, positive voice—one that listens, acts, and delivers real results. As a young candidate, I bring fresh energy, new ideas, and a determination to make local politics work better for everyone.

I’ve lived in Crawley all my life, attended local schools, and worked in the area, so I understand first-hand the issues that matter most to our community.

The Liberal Democrats stand for fairness, opportunity, and strong local communities. I share those values, and I’m committed to putting them into action here in Crawley.

My priorities for Langley Green & Tushmore are:
• Stronger local services – ensuring our streets are clean, safe, and well-maintained
• Affordable housing – pushing for more genuinely affordable homes for local people
• Protecting our environment – improving green spaces and tackling pollution
• Supporting young people – creating better opportunities, skills, and community support
• Listening to residents – being visible, accessible, and accountable all year round

I believe politics should be about people—not parties. That means working hard, listening to your concerns, and standing up for our community at every opportunity.

This election is your chance to choose a representative who will speak up for you and work tirelessly on your behalf. If elected, I will always put Langley Green & Tushmore first.

Thank you for your support.

Kemuel Thompson

Liberal Democrat Candidate for Langley Green & Tushmore (and Ifield East)

Cavan Wood grew up in Horsted Keynes and has made Lindfield his home for the last 30 years, with both his children attending local village schools.

Cavan has represented Lindfield on the District Council and on the Parish Council, where he has worked to protect our local environment and green space.

As a retired teacher who worked at local schools, Cavan is committed to getting a fair deal for students.

He has consistently raised the issue of potholes, flooding and road maintenance. Cavan backed the proposal from Liberal Democrat County Councillors for extra road repairs funding, which the Conservatives opposed.

Emma Barker describes herself as an eco-feminist and works as a secondary school teacher of Spanish, French and Italian. During her last position at Imberhorne School, East Grinstead, she set up an eco-club for the children so they could plant vegetables (used by the catering staff), tend to chickens, be citizen scientists for the big butterfly count and plant trees donated by the Woodland Trust. The school achieved distinction as a Green School. Since 2023, she had run the youth group for Climate commitment, meeting termly with local MP Mims Davies, so teenagers can raise local and global issues. She also contributed to her local church achieving a bronze Eco church award.

“I care deeply about Lindfield and the High Weald because this isn’t just a place on a map — it’s a community, a landscape and a way of life that so many of us value. From our historic villages to the countryside that surrounds them, it’s an area worth protecting and investing in. If elected to West Sussex County Council, I want to work hard to make sure our communities are properly heard, that we secure the support and services we need, and that we achieve more for the people and places that make this area so special.”

Dr Eva Johanna Holmberg lives in Littlehampton with her husband and her senior bunny Ned. A Finnish historian of early modern Britain, mobility and migration, Eva Johanna is a passionate defender of human rights, equality, and the environment.

As a county councillor, she would secure more funding for adult social care and cost of living support to tackle anti-social behaviour and homelessness, and to fight for our schools, SEND provision, libraries, and museums. All this would build our resilience and support the positive changes already under way in Littlehampton.

When the conservative government tried to remove 117 legally residing EU citizens from the UK in 2017, including Dr Holmberg, she was the leader in bringing this to the attention of the media, forcing a U-turn and an apology from Theresa May. This is the ‘eldest daughter’ energy she brings into anything she does.

Anita was elected as a Green councillor for Arun District Council in May 2023, she was then co-opted to Aldingbourne Parish Council in February 2024. Growing up in West Sussex and spending her teenage years walking and camping in West Sussex fostered a love and respect for the flora and fauna of this beautiful area and the community throughout. Anita has worked in Littlehampton since 2000, with a break of about six years working in Eastbourne.

Previously Anita worked eleven years at The Economist as a Graphic Designer, three years learning joinery in a workshop in Brighton and ten years working in the Organic Food sector, becoming involved with Traders Association and many community projects. Anita is passionate about Organic farming. She believes it is the only way to save our soil, ourselves, and our future, and would love Sussex to be a pesticide free county.

Cyril has lived and worked in Crawley for over 50 years and has a vast history of campaigning in the town for many important causes. He joined the Green Party in 1987 after meeting Petra Kelly, a founding member of the German Green Party, at Glastonbury Festival. He has been involved with environmental campaigns most of his adult life such as save the whales and stop the fur trade.

Cyril is a member of the RSPB, Whale and Dolphin Conservation, Sussex Wildlife Trust, Woodland Trust, Bumblebee Conservation Trust and Amnesty International. He is a long-time gardener and allotmenteer.

“I will provide a voice of reason and responsibility on the Council, helping make sure every decision has the climate emergency, the health and wellbeing of Crawley people at the heart of it.”
I’ve lived and worked in the Midhurst area most of my life. I am actively involved in many community organisations determined for our concerns about nature, pollution and improving  people’s lives.

I am delighted to have been selected as the Libertarian Party’s candidate for Midhurst. I am a geography teacher and live near Rogate.

I am standing to cut council tax - returning more of our hard-earned cash to our pockets - to protect our night skies and fix the potholes blighting our roads.

There is no such thing as "public" money - only taxpayers' money. Cutting tax is the most essential service local government can provide during a cost of living crisis. I have identified savings to fund a 3% council tax reduction. All other candidates in this election are promising to ringfence or expand services. All I can promise you is your hard-earned money back.

As an amateur astronomer, I appreciate the value of pristine, unpolluted skies. Unfortunately, the South Downs Dark Skies Reserve appears to exist only on paper, as increasing levels of light pollution wash out our view of the Milky Way and disturb nocturnal fauna.

Light pollution does not respect boundaries. Even if lighting within the South Downs National Park is well controlled, skyglow from surrounding towns brightens the night sky across the entire region. In March this year, West Sussex County Council announced a £24 million spend on new street lighting, claiming to have considered light pollution while relying on specifications that use 4000K “cool white” LEDs — bluer than recommended for dark sky areas and operating at wavelengths known to increase skyglow. A council that is home to a Dark Sky Reserve should not be spending millions of taxpayers’ money actively undermining it.

As your councillor, I will press the council to turn off unnecessary illumination and adopt lower intensity warm-colour LEDs (≤3000K) as standard for replacements. This will cut carbon emissions, save money, and protect our view of the cosmos.

Fixing potholes is a basic responsibility of local government, yet our roads are neglected. The council spends time and resources on other priorities, such as interfering in the market by

I’m excited to be part of the Green Party’s movement of hope and unity. I will work hard to address issues for Worthing residents and nurture our community. I love living in Worthing, and becoming a mum recently made me look at our town with a fresh outlook. I’m committed to making Worthing a greener, safer and happier place for our children to grow up in.

"

I am Nick Wiltshire, your local Liberal Democrat candidate for Castle Ward. I live and work locally, so I use the same facilities and roads that you do, including pot-holes. Working in healthcare means listening to others, helping others, and working as a team, qualities I would bring to Worthing’s Council, if elected.

I am a passionate environmentalist and want to make Worthing a kinder, greener and more sustainable place – planting more urban trees, recycling more types of plastic, making our transport system greener and ensuring that our bathing waters are clean enough to enjoy for swimming, surfing or sailing!"

[taken from: https://www.wor-ld.org.uk/our-team/nick-wiltshire]

I’ve called Crawley home for six years. In that time, I’ve watched a proud town decline. Those in power caused it—and now expect your trust again. Enough is enough. I’m standing to fix our town centre, make streets safer, sort the roads, and hold them to account.

Broadfield is my home, I have lived here for my entire life and it has been the greatest privilege  to have represented our neighbourhood on the council for the last three years. I have always put our community’s interests first and will continue to do so if re-elected.

I have been active in our residents’ association, Talk Broadfield, for many years as it is important to listen to residents’ voices.

I will always be available, accessible and happy to assist with issues affecting the area. I hope I can count on your vote to ensure Broadfield continues to have a councillor who truly cares.

Ben has lived in Crawley for 6 Years. He works in the transport sector and has been a trade union representative and activist for a long time.

He is a keen cyclist and environmentalist who loves the amount of green spaces Crawley and the surrounding area have to offer as well the wonderful and diverse local community.

Since moving to Crawley Ben has always wanted to see more voices on the left in prominent positions, pushing issues like the cost of living and proper maintenance of our public spaces.

“Crawley is a wonderful place to live but there are improvements that need to be made. I want to see local residents have the, affordable, frequent and reliable public transport they deserve. With well lit bus shelters at every stop. I’m fed up dodging the same potholes every day on my bike. I want to see better accountability from the people who run our councils.“

Carol Birch has quietly changed the face of local politics in Arun.

Her calm, determined approach helped elect the first ever Green councillors to Arun District Council in 2019 and she’s done it again in two by-elections since.

A social housing specialist and wildlife gardener, Carol brings the same thoroughness to everything she does: fighting for people who need a home, and protecting the natural spaces that make this area worth living in.

She’s one of the people who built the local Green Party into what it is today!

Tim has lived in Petworth for two years, and has previously lived in both Pulborough and Storrington. He is a semi-retired piano tuner, and has also worked as music teacher and musician, having done these three things for all his working life.

Tim is a committed environmentalist, and supports Friends Of The Earth, Greenpeace and The Green Party, and is of course a member of the Green Party.

Tim is also a committed Christian, and attends a church in Storrington, often playing piano in the worship team for services.

If elected, Tim’s focus as Councillor will of course be to concentrate on issues vital to local people, such as: The wellbeing of our farming community and associated businesses; The safeguarding of local services; Paying close heed to any proposed developments such as new houses, or proposed new roads, and to balance the sometimes conflicting needs of different sections of the local community; Keeping business rates for local shops and offices as low as possible; Conserving the wonderful historic architecture within the historic town of Petworth

Nick is a former borough and county councillor, having previously served as Crawley’s Chair of Housing. He currently volunteers as Vice Chair of Governors at a local primary school. 

If elected, Nick will work with Cllr Justin Russell to address residents’ priorities, such as the problems with developers in Forge Wood and the maintenance of Milton Mount flats.

Professionally, Nick works to combat financial crime and in his spare time he is an avid Crawley Town supporter, cheering the team on at games across the country.

Born and bred in West Sussex and moved to the Three Bridges / Pound Hill area in 2022, with my husband, daughter and dog, after spending a few years travelling abroad. I am passionate about strengthening the local community, bringing people together, and ensuring that decisions made at council level reflect the needs of residents.

“I believe it’s time for a change; I’m tired of our community services diminishing, potholes not being fixed, litter everywhere, homelessness rising, the cost of greed crisis. We have a rich and vibrant community; it’s time we start enjoying and embracing it again.”

Here is some background. I have had 10 years army experience, serving as Captain Owen-Burge and completing two tours of Afghanistan. I live in the district with my wife and two young children and have strong connections with West Sussex.

At home, I like to think of myself as a dedicated and hands-on dad. At work, I am a hard-working project manager with a focus on humanitarian support, so I really know how to get things done for people. As well as all that, I have had time to make a difference to others’ lives, from raising funds for local nature reserves and improving the local Girl Guides hut to campaigning for a ‘search and rescue’ team to provide essential equipment. I am proudest of speaking up for a local family with a disabled child facing eviction.

Now I want to take that same commitment to County Hall.

It’s vitally important to support our local businesses. Lower Street businesses, and all businesses locally are struggling with rising prices and the fiasco caused by the extended closure of the A29 at Church Hill in December 2022. The A29 was closed for too long, with WSCC so slow off the mark and Broomers Hill residents suffering intolerable rat-running. While at least the road is now open, WSCC has not come out of this smelling of roses locally. The impact of inaction has been widespread. If I’d been able to fight your corner, it could’ve been so different!

Pulborough, West Chiltington and all the local villages have so much to offer but businesses need a strong voice to speak up for each area’s differing needs and I am that voice! Things are tough for local people with rising prices, poor bus services, terrible roads and too many houses in the wrong places.

There’s a clear choice at this election. A vote for Reform, with their politics of division and anger - or you can choose a positive future by voting for me. I offer experience and a proven track record of speaking up. I am a strong voice you can trust. I love working hard to help people and to ma

I've lived with my family in West Chiltington for over 6 years and have run the local Liberal Democrat party across Horsham District for 5 years.

I understand the area having spoken to numerrous residents about their local issues and I understand how local government works

I have a brackground in business large and small and have coached a number of business leaders

Now I want to act for my community here in Pulborough to ensure that we get the representation that we need

VOTE FOR WILLIAM ASHTON AS COUNTY COUNCILLOR FOR PULBOROUGH

Will is a retired solicitor and life long socialist who has supported and campaigned for the Labour Party for more than 50 years. He has a firm grasp of the local politics of West Sussex and supports the causes of the disadvantaged and often neglected members of our society.

His policies include:-
• Action on traffic and air pollution and homelessness;
• Installing traffic calming and safe walking and cycling routes;
• Building the homes our young people need, while defending the countryside.
• Setting up a partnership with the CLT to build this urgently-needed affordable housing.

Also;
• Promoting the Arts and Recreation.
• Supporting services to help refugees find work and to give their time to voluntary groups
• Defending the South Downs National Park.
• Encouraging local businesses and employment initiatives.
• Reversing the cuts in youth and community projects

Read more at:

Website: https://southdownslabour.org.uk/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/southdownslabour
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/asd_labour/

Contact details: southdownslabour@gmail.com

Published and promoted by William Ashton on behalf of the Arundel & South Downs Constituency Labour Party and the candidate all at Toro, Lake Lane, Barnham PO22 0JB

© 2026 copyright Arundel & South Downs Labour All rights reserved.

My name is Tom Richardson and you may know me as the landlord of the Three Moles Pub in Selham, near Graffham that my partner and I took on to renovate and improve after I completed 15 years’ service as a police officer with Surrey Police. It’s not easy running any business at the moment but I am proud of what we’ve achieved, including winning multiple awards for sustainability and West Sussex pub of the year. A lot of that is about hard work and getting things done.

Public service and putting something back are important to me and so after leaving the police, I have represented our community on West Sussex County Council. I have served too in the past on both Graffham and Lodsworth Parish Councils.

I am standing for re-election on 7th May this year and before all the leaflets start flying through your letterbox, I wanted to take this opportunity to write to you personally.

Why am I standing?
It’s not easy or fashionable standing for council these days but I believe it’s important we have a strong voice. Our rural village communities are too easily over-looked in favour of cities like Chichester or Brighton yet our roads, village schools and bus services need active support. There is a lot more to do but I’ve used my own policing background to work with Sussex Police on better enforcement against noisy and anti-social road users and rural fly-tippers.  A big part of this was leading the review and re-writing of the county’s speed limit policy that now gives communities the option to apply for more appropriate speed limits in their villages and outside schools that previously would not have been possible.  And I’ve helped get our county council to spend record amounts on practical things like fixing pot-holes, road resurfacing and drainage issues along with the introduction of the book-a-bus service which gives our rural communities that vital transport link that has been missing for years.

I believe we deserve a councillor who listens, who lives locally, is easi

Ginny has been associated with this area all of her life and loves this special corner of West Sussex. She went to school in Duncton, Easebourne and Midhurst and has been on the governing bodies of local schools as well as on the board of two local charities. Ginny has worked as an NHS consultant in Sussex Hospitals for over 20 years. Prior to that she was a lecturer/senior lecturer at a teaching hospital in London.

Ginny cares deeply about our local wild places and has volunteered in them since the 1980s. Many people are doing superb work locally, nevertheless she is concerned that Nature and people are being put at risk as a result of pollution of our air, rivers and land. She have been campaigning for several years to raise awareness of local, national and international issues.

The fact that some political parties put profit before the protection of people and wildlife has motivated her to stand for election - protecting our precious environment has surely got to be worth a try.

She says "The People’s Emergency Briefing tells us that 88% of people living in England and Wales want more done regarding global overheating. Our ability to grow food is at risk, the south east of England will be the most impacted by water shortages, people are waking up to the fact that we need nature to thrive if we are to survive. Let’s forget our differences over the smaller things and come together to fix our situation whilst we still can. The window of opportunity is closing fast. If we put our minds to it, step up and do the responsible thing I know we can do it. You never know we might even enjoy it."
I am a local Councillor currently who is passionate about our village and maintaining local services.

Gavin is a former District Councillor (Norfolk in the 80’s), now living in Yapton, or Yaptown as it is now being called, due to it’s ever increasing size, with very many of the not very affordable houses being built on flood plains. He was the Green candidate in the Chichester constituency in the 2021 general election.

Gavin is looking forward to representing the residents of the Rustington area on WSCC.

Claire, originally from Dorset, has strong ties to the local area, having worked in Selsey for nine years before making it her home in 2018.

She is a Chartered Accountant and runs her own business from her home in Selsey, supporting small and growing businesses with their finances. Alongside her partner, she built the business to help local business owners succeed and strengthen the local economy.

Adrienne Lowe has lived in Widewater in Lancing for more than 35 years.  A recently retired teacher, she is keen to represent local people and to reflect their concerns on issues such as flooding, sewage spills and housing.  She supports the development of New Salts Farm into a thriving natural habitat. She says: “not only can the area once again be a wildlife haven, but it can also improve the natural flood defences”.

Adrienne is chair of a local charity, Adur Voluntary Action, which encourages volunteers to get involved in the community. She is known for her honesty and integrity and will work tirelessly to represent the residents of Widewater.

Local Election, St Mary's Ward

I am standing for election in St Mary's Ward to bring the Green Party's message of hope and unity to Adur District Council.
I believe that Adur District Council needs more Green voices influencing decsions that affect local people. There should be  collaboration between the people who are elected to represent our area based on the needs of all residents rather than serving internal party politics. Greens are free to speak up and represent the views of local people; they are not told how to vote by the national party
I will work hard for Shoreham to become a greener, kinder, fairer place for all who live and work here. Hope can become normal again.

West Sussex County Council Elections, Shoreham North

I am standing for the Shoreham North Division for West Sussex County Council as I believe that having more Green county councillors will lead to better representation of everyday people.  Greens are free to speak up for residents and are not told how to vote by the national party.  I will work hard all year round,  not just at election time, to find out what matters to local people and ensure that concerns about county council matters, such as the state of the roads, transport, social care and education are heard and dealt with.  The two party political system is broken and it is time to have Greens making hope normal again in local government.

I have been a District counselor in Saint Nic's Ward for two years and I am now standing as candidate for County Council because I believe there's a lot more that can be done at this level for the people.of Shoreham South. This includes everything to do with highways education and social services. If elected I will work hard to represent the people of Shoreham South to get progress on these matters, as West Sussex County Council has been letting us all down badly for many years.

I’m standing as your local Green candidate because I believe in a fairer, greener future where everyone feels they belong. I stand for bringing people together and pushing back against division and extremism. We have far more in common than what sets us apart. I’m committed to protecting and expanding access to green spaces, environmental education, and building a stronger, more connected community across Adur, starting with Manor Ward.

My career has been in software engineering. I've worked in events media, in finance and currently in defence where I manage a project, architect the solutions and write the code. I've spent the last 20 years facing complex problems and solving them. I have been living in Crawley since 2016 and lived in Surrey before that.

I voted Leave in the 2016 referendum. I've never regretted that vote nor the following times we had to hammer the point home to our political class. Like so many others I voted for Boris in the hope that we had an optimistic, British patriot who would champion our people, our country and our values and reverse the downward trends we all experienced. Like so many others I have now seen what the Conservative party has become.

I will never give up on Britain, her values, people and history. With a strong reform of our institutions, we will save Britain.

My priorities are focussed on public safety, the safety of women and girls and to stop the rampant crime that is happening through Crawley.  Spending is out of control and that needs to be brought back to sensible levels and only on what benefits the good people of Crawley.  There are a myriad of other issues to resolve as well, such as restoring the high street, get council house repairs done as close to first time as possible, stop West of Ifield expansion and resist the illegal immigrant settlement into our communities putting our safety at great risk.  None of us at Reform are single issue, we hear all perspectives and record all problems raised to us for addressing.

Oh, and fix the damn potholes.

Zack Ali got married in 2009 and made Crawley his home. Since then, he has been a resident of Broadfield. Zack is a proud father of two young boys, both of whom went to Hilltop Primary School in Southgate.  He has received an honours degree in Business management and finance from University of Surrey.

In 2007 he worked for Domino’s Pizza and quickly ended up managing several stores across Sussex and Surrey. He was based at the store on Crawley High Street and led a team of store managers from across the region and was responsible for more than 200 staff. The Crawley store became one of the top 10 performing stores worldwide, whilst under Zack’s leadership. He went on to start his own business, this grew it to the point where it employed over 50 people and served thousands of customers every year.

Zack was elected to Crawley Borough Council and West Sussex County Council in 2021, representing Southgate and Gossops Green division of Crawley. He has sat on several committees and is a member of the shadow cabinet for Planning and Economic development on Crawley Borough Council.

He has been actively involved in Crawley politics since 2007, having worked alongside Henry Smith MP on various local issues and campaigns. Zack has also played a key role in several general election campaigns supporting Henry Smith MP to win the Crawley seat.

He has worked with a number local and national charities in support of a range charitable causes and issues. Including during the pandemic, where he gathered several likeminded people to set up a group to support vulnerable local residents. This group did grocery and food shopping for shielding families, vulnerable households and those on low incomes.

Zack also joined the NHS Volunteer Responders and helped local communities across Crawley to stay safe and feel supported. Taking part in activities such as welfare phone calls with residents living alone and dropping of medication and care packages.

Iain Dickson (Gossops Green) is a long serving member of the Green Party and an even longer serving member of the Crawley community. Iain was also the Green Party prospective parliamentary candidate for Crawley and stood in the 2019 and 2025 General election gaining Crawley Green Party’s best ever result!

“I am standing as the Green Party candidate in Southgate and Gossops Green to help people and to improve our community by putting people and the environment first. As a councillor, I believe I can help and improve the area through better Council provision and community action.” - Iain Dickson

My name is Jen Nuin Smith. I have lived in the south of Horsham for over 10 years. I’m standing in the upcoming Horsham District Council by-election to represent Denne ward and am writing to ask for your support on Thursday 21st November.

Here are some reasons why you may consider voting for me:

As a qualified teacher and founder of an eco-democratic learning community for children, I have the skills to do the job.

Protecting this beautiful part of Horsham we live in, for residents and for generations to come, is my main priority. Denne has an historical past, a positive future and is a great place to bring up children, all of which need protecting. As your Green councillor I will use my organisational skills to look at the detail of planning applications and at policies which will affect the area. As mother to an inquisitive nine-year-old son, and an educator, giving young people a voice is very important to me. I always work hard and like to get the job done. I’m determined to ensure my young son and all our children grow up to a future worth having.I am hard working, careful and dedicated to conserving our local environment.

The time is right for some change in Denne.

In Denne we have a Lib Dem MP, we have a Lib Dem-run Horsham District Council and we already have two Lib Dem councillors here to represent us. Isn’t it time for some change?

One strong, independently minded Green councillor could do so much for the area. We already have 9 hard working Green councillors speaking up for residents at Horsham District Council.

Your vote could make that 10 Green councillors - all working hard for the issues that matter to residents like speaking up about local issues and working hard to protect and connect our community.

The job of a county council is to work for its residents in managing and delivering properly budgeted local public services. This is not happening in West Sussex.   Tory-led West Sussex County Council has become complacent and ineffective.  We are now faced with a massive shortfall in available funding for our services, due not only to the pandemic and government cuts but, in part, created by the catalogue of failings and financial mismanagement by our Council over the years.    These failings are well documented. They include a botched tendering process costing £4 million, large pay-outs to the former Chief Executive, and a recent decision to spend over £15 million on retail units in Broadbridge Heath at a time when retail is becoming an increasingly risky investment and this money is needed to help keep those front line public services we need.   Not only are we now faced with the recently announced cuts to our Family and Children’s Services, but future service reductions in key areas seem likely at a time of increased demand.  This will include schools, affecting our children’s education outcomes, and adult social services affecting care for our older people.  These are services we can’t afford to lose.     Labour councillors are working hard and doing a good job in highlighting Tory failings, standing up for our services and calling the Tories to account. Our councillors even presented a costed budget, which the Tories refused to support, but which would have safeguarded a lot more of the services now at risk.   To ensure that the Council prioritises high quality, funded local services for our community, we need more Labour councillors. If you want to make a difference, vote Labour in the County Council elections.    Chris Henson

I am a life-long Adur resident as well as a parent and heritage worker.

I am passionate about my environment and to preserving the things which make our area special, including the sea, the River Adur and the Downs. I am also committed to ensuring effective services which meet the needs of all residents, as well as to planning for a more equitable and sustainable future.

Tony Hogben – A Local Voice for Our Community

I’m Tony Hogben, a local resident, businessman and community campaigner who has proudly served as a District Councillor for over a decade.

My family and I live locally and run Horsham Swim School, where we teach hundreds of local children every week to swim safely and confidently. Running a local business gives me a real understanding of the pressures facing families, businesses and community groups.

I am honoured to have served as Cabinet Member and Deputy Leader of Horsham District Council. I have also completed the Local Government Association Leadership Programme at Warwick University. Combined with over a decade of experience as a councillor, I believe this gives me strong local knowledge and practical experience.

I’m standing with Reform because I believe in common-sense policies, protecting our local communities and putting residents first.

If elected to West Sussex County Council, I will stand up for local people and work hard for our community

Claire first moved to Horsham in 2001 after she graduated and worked for Chemovation and then Novartis who she relocated with to Boston, Massachusetts for six years. She returned to the area in 2021 following the birth of her daughter Maddie and now works at a research fellow at Sussex University conducting cancer research.

She is a member of the Sussex Wildlife Trust and the RSPB. The Sussex countryside and its beauty are very important to Claire.

Claire says a very strong no to Crawsham - the house building in the area is excessive and unacceptable. Claire opposes any plans for a Horsham incinerator and is speaking up for a hospital with an A&E.

Claire appreciates that some new houses are needed - but they should be affordable, sustainable and not built in the strategic gap between Horsham and Crawley. Any development that does go ahead must have adequate infrastructure at its core, as Horsham schools, roads and GP’s surgeries are already under significant pressure.

I'm John Loney, your local Green candidate for West Sussex County Council in the Storrington division area. It's been wonderful getting things done for our community over the past years and meeting so many of you more recently. Thank you for your continued support.
