I’m a local man who understands local people and I have the skills to do the job. I'm from Storrington. I have lived here for several years. I love the place and the people, but I do get fed up when I see that the whole of the area, from Parham to Amberley, though Storrington, across Ashington and Washington to Wiston is often ignored and neglected. I am keen to really make a difference to local people's lives. That's why I became a Parish Councillor at Storrington & Sullington Parish Council and was voted in as Vice Chair last year. I’m hard working, organised and know how to get things done.

As your West Sussex County councillor, I'll get even more done for our area.

I am always free to speak up for local residents. Unlike other parties, the Green Party doesn't tell councillors how to vote. I can always put you ahead of party politics and stand up for what's right for Storrington, Sullington, Cootham, Amberley, Ashington, Washington, Parham and Wiston.

In recent months, as part of my Parish Council responsibilities, I've worked hard to make our streets safer, to reduce air pollution, sort out footpaths and write bids for funding to improve our leisure facilities. I've reported endless potholes and followed up to get them fixed and I've helped support the Storrington White Stork and Nature Festival (at Chanctonbury Leisure Centre on 23rd May) and the revival of the Friends of Meadowside group and I am currently working on a playground strategy.

You face an important choice this May. The truth is, the old parties you've perhaps always voted for can't win round here this time.

I can win here in Storrington division. At the last local elections, the Greens won first place in Storrington. The Conservat

Alex is passionate about creating a green and sustainable future for our community. He has campaigned to increase local train services, and supports the Liberal Democrat's campaign to prevent the closure of the Billingshurst recycling unit. In addition, he is part of a local litter picking group and believes more should be done to support safe cycle networks. He works as a journalist specialising in finance and has launched a sustainable finance group for some of Europe's largest pension funds - bringing together experts who will finance our future.

Alex, his wife and their three young children live in the local area, along with his extended family. He values local identity and, as a Parish Councillor, works to conserve the neighbourhood. Alex has personal experience of local services including education and provision for the elderly. He wants to ensure everyone has the opportunity and freedom to pursue their goals regardless of who they are and where they come from.

After over 30 years of Education and Leadership I want to stand and deliver for the people of Tarring. I strongly believe that we have a duty to look after local people and ensure our council tax is used efficiently to improve services and the area we live in.

Cloe is a Worthing resident whose work and life is rooted in community work. She works in Learning Disabilities and has volunteered for various initiatives such as after school clubs. She believes the people of Tarring deserve better representation. The community in Tarring deserves to have its green spaces protected, functioning and safe roads, support for the vulnerable and someone who uplifts their voices.

Vote for Cloe if you want someone who cares for the community in Tarring.

Tarring’s voice won’t be lost or forgotten with Cloe.

As someone who grew up locally on a small holding in Sidlesham, Andrew knows the value of local farming and horticulture. As a teenager he was one of the first group of volunteer wardens at Pagham Harbour local nature reserve. He feels privileged to live in such a beautiful part of the country, enjoys sailing in Chichester harbour and swimming in the sea at the Witterings. Andrew is passionate about conserving the diversity of our natural environment and hopes we can keep it that way for future generations to enjoy.

Paul moved to Three Bridges in 2008 and his children attended Three Bridges Primary.

Paul has worked as a software engineer for many years including for The Office of the Public Guardian (part of the Ministry of Justice) and also HM Courts and Tribunals Service. Through those projects he developed a passion for working for the public good. He also gained first-hand experience of how complex and challenging government work is, and how vital it is to get it right.

He is a long term supporter of Crawley Open House.

“I’m tired of seeing politicians talking about ‘cutting costs’ when ‘costs’ are essential public services like refuse collection, road maintenance, and schools. Three Bridges is the heart of Crawley. It needs people who care enough about democracy to be available, listen carefully to the citizens, and accurately represent their needs at the county level. Me and my fellow Greens are those people.”

I have lived in Crawley for over 40 years and have a wonderful family of 2 beautiful girls and a son who now lives in Japan.

Since I arrived in Crawley my vision was to settle down and retire in the town that has given me the most. During this period I have operated several businesses in the area, most of them had direct contact with the local residents. I understand the pressing needs of residents.

As I am very passionate about the community and the Town I will work very hard to promote positive change needed to improve the community.

In my final 5 working years I have worked in Local Government and understand the operations of the local council and this will speed up any changes required for the community.

I am looking forward to serving the community of Crawley.

James is a freelance musician living in Balcombe. He is the Choir Director at Holy Trinity, Hurstpierpoint, as well as a composer and theatre musical director. He is a passionate advocate for the arts and committed to creating inclusive creative and cultural opportunities, something he has worked towards in recent years as an arts charity trustee.

Previously a full-time teacher, he has an understanding of the specific challenges facing parents and teachers, particularly in the area of SEND provision. He has run outreach projects to several primary and secondary schools and has experienced first-hand the need for better funding and facilities, particularly in supporting the facilitation of individual skills and interests.

His professional training includes Keeping Children Safe in Education and other aspects of child safety and protection, first aid and fire safety, and courses on ADHD, mental wellbeing in children and young people, and online safety.

“I’m standing as the Green Party candidate for Worth Forest because I care deeply about our community and the future we’re building together here in West Sussex. Too often, local voices are overlooked while decisions are made without enough focus on long-term sustainability or the everyday needs of residents. I want to change that by championing practical, people-focused solutions—protecting our local environment, improving transport links, supporting community services, and ensuring development works for everyone. I believe in listening to residents, so that together we can create a healthier, fairer, and more resilient Worth Forest.”

With nearly a decade of unwavering commitment to the Green movement, I’m standing in East Worthing to offer a beacon of principled politics and genuine hope. Too many people feel unrepresented and unheard, left cold by hateful discourse and divisive rhetoric. I have a degree in Politics and International Relations and have spent years volunteering for causes I believe in. I want to be that alternative for the people of East Worthing — representing the area with compassion, force and an unwavering commitment to those I represent.

Natasha grew up in Worthing, and studied at Davison High School and Worthing Sixth Form, before leaving to study and work internationally. She holds a doctorate from Oxford and has spent her career working in business strategy and international development, including improving livelihoods for smallholder farmers and expanding access to medicines in low-income countries.

Now back home, Natasha is standing for Central Ward on Worthing Borough Council and Pier Division on West Sussex County Council. She brings practical experience of working on issues that matter to real people, and the independence to speak up for Worthing residents without fear or favour.

Martin McCabe was born and raised in Worthing, Martin's goals are simple: to empower our community and demand a better politics that puts people first. A Building Engineer and lecturer, Martin is obsessed with making sure that politicians get the details right.

Martin has a strong record of standing up for local people. In 2018, he helped a local special needs charity find a new home so its work could continue. He has raised funds for local disability and women's football teams and campaigned for a People's Vote on Brexit. In May 2019, he is running the 100km London to Brighton ultra marathon to raise money for West Sussex Mind. Martin is the Chairman of the Adur and Worthing Liberal Democrats and our candidate for Tarring ward in the 2019 Worthing Council Elections.

I am standing in the Marine Ward by-election for Worthing Borough Council, held on Thursday 19 September. 

Having grown up in Worthing, I deeply cherish our town and feel grateful every day to live by the seaside. Whether I’m enjoying long walks on the beach with my dog or kayaking along the coast, the beauty of Worthing always reminds me of how special our community is. This is why I’m committed to ensuring our town is well-governed and flourishing - the decline of our town under Labour is what motivated me to stand in this election. Whether it is the year-on-year increases in Council Tax or the lack of street cleaning in our local area, I know that Worthing is being let down by Labour. We need a Council that delivers for residents and if elected I will work every day to ensure that the Council works for you.

Having spent many years working in the private sector, including senior management roles I bring a strong track record of leadership, accountability, and delivery. 
I care deeply about our local community and bring energy, enthusiasm, and a proactive approach to delivering the real change residents are calling for. People deserve a council that listens, takes action, and puts local priorities first.
My top priorities are:
•	Revitalising the town centre, celebrating local heritage, and protecting small businesses
•	Improving the condition of our roads
•	Tackling crime, antisocial behaviour, and shoplifting
•	Protecting our coastline and safeguarding green spaces
•	Managing major projects responsibly and ensuring value for money in council spending

Only Reform UK is offering the bold change needed locally. 
Labour tried to take away your democratic right to vote - Lend me your vote to make real positive changes for all of Worthing.

I have been living in West Worthing for eight years and am passionate about the local area and what it has to offer the local residents – if only it was cared for by the people in charge of building its fairer communities, keeping its water supplies clean, fixing the potholes, and keeping Worthing a safe town for future generations to grow up in. As someone who has two jobs and still worries about paying the bills on time every month, I know the challenges and worries of everyday life and will work my hardest to keep this town special and help it grow.
